name: Langtrace
tagline: Open source OpenTelemetry observability for LLM applications
metaDescription: Langtrace is an open source, OpenTelemetry-based tool that captures traces and metrics from your LLM, vector database, and framework calls. Self-host or use the cloud.
overview: Langtrace is an open source observability tool for applications that call LLM APIs, vector databases, and LLM frameworks. You add its SDK to your code, and it captures traces and metrics that let you debug and analyze what your application is doing at runtime.

It is built for developers building LLM-powered apps who need to see latency, cost, and usage patterns instead of guessing. The traces follow OpenTelemetry (OTEL) standards, so the data fits into tooling you may already use.

As an LLM observability tool, Langtrace sits between your app and the model or database providers. It automatically captures traces from many vendors and frameworks, and you can run it as a managed cloud service or self-host the whole stack on your own infrastructure.
features: Built on OpenTelemetry (OTEL) standards for consistent, portable tracing
Real-time monitoring of LLM API calls, vector database operations, and framework usage
Performance insights into latency, cost, and usage patterns
Automatic trace capture for many LLM providers, frameworks, and vector databases (OpenAI, Anthropic, Langchain, LlamaIndex, Pinecone, ChromaDB, and more)
Self-hosting option via Docker Compose, with no telemetry leaving your servers

gettingStarted.intro: Install the SDK for your language, then initialize Langtrace before importing any LLM modules. You can use the managed cloud or self-host the stack.

steps.heading: Install the SDK
steps.body: Install the Langtrace SDK for TypeScript/JavaScript or Python.
steps.code:
# TypeScript/JavaScript
npm i @langtrase/typescript-sdk

# Python
pip install langtrace-python-sdk

steps.heading: Initialize in TypeScript
steps.body: Import and initialize Langtrace before any LLM module imports. Pass your API key, or set LANGTRACE_API_KEY as an environment variable and call init() with no arguments.
steps.code:
import * as Langtrace from '@langtrase/typescript-sdk' // Must precede any llm module imports
Langtrace.init({ api_key: '<your_api_key>' })

steps.heading: Initialize in Python
steps.body: Import langtrace and call init() with your API key, or set LANGTRACE_API_KEY in the environment and call init() with no arguments.

steps.heading: Self-host with Docker (optional)
steps.body: To run Langtrace locally you need the Next.js app, a Postgres database, and a Clickhouse database. With Docker and Docker Compose installed, start the stack and open http://localhost:3000.

useCases: Debugging an LLM application by tracing each step of a workflow to see where it slows down or fails
Tracking the cost and latency of LLM API calls across providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini
Monitoring vector database operations (Pinecone, ChromaDB, Weaviate) alongside model calls in one trace
Self-hosting observability for LLM apps where data must stay on your own infrastructure
